title: prunus armeniaca subsp. dasycarpa
intro: Prunus armeniaca subsp. dasycarpa, also known as the Mongolian apricot, is a subspecies of apricot native to Central Asia, characterized by its small to medium-sized fruits and edible stones. The bark and leaves of the Mongolian apricot have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Prunus armeniaca subsp. dasycarpa include reducing inflammation, fighting oxidative stress, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a popular ingredient in traditional folk medicine.
